Fan Experiment Analysis - extra

JD

2025-02-04

library(tidyverse)
library(tidybayes)
library(brms)
library(bayesplot)
dotproduct <- read_csv("../csv_files/dotproducts_perword.csv")
## Rows: 7152 Columns: 6
## ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (1): Word
## dbl (4): Dot_product, Model_seed, n_epochs, List
## lgl (1): Location_fan
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
actr <- read_csv("../csv_files/actr_activations_perword.csv")
## Rows: 2400 Columns: 4
## ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (1): Word
## dbl (2): ACT_R_activation, List
## lgl (1): Location_fan
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
print(dotproduct)
## # A tibble: 7,152 × 6
##    Word       Dot_product Location_fan Model_seed n_epochs  List
##    <chr>            <dbl> <lgl>             <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl>
##  1 plantenkas       1.37  TRUE                 23       65     1
##  2 terras           1.01  TRUE                 23       65     1
##  3 tram             1.93  TRUE                 23       65     1
##  4 metro            0.474 TRUE                 23       65     1
##  5 afgrond          1.07  TRUE                 23       65     1
##  6 bus              0.957 TRUE                 23       65     1
##  7 trein            0.779 TRUE                 23       65     1
##  8 tempel           1.47  TRUE                 23       65     1
##  9 filmhuis         2.36  TRUE                 23       65     1
## 10 synagoge         0.947 TRUE                 23       65     1
## # ℹ 7,142 more rows
print(actr)
## # A tibble: 2,400 × 4
##    Word       ACT_R_activation Location_fan  List
##    <chr>                 <dbl> <lgl>        <dbl>
##  1 plantenkas             128. TRUE             1
##  2 terras                 121. TRUE             1
##  3 tram                   123. TRUE             1
##  4 metro                  128. TRUE             1
##  5 afgrond                129. TRUE             1
##  6 bus                    121. TRUE             1
##  7 trein                  120. TRUE             1
##  8 tempel                 127. TRUE             1
##  9 filmhuis               133. TRUE             1
## 10 synagoge               126. TRUE             1
## # ℹ 2,390 more rows

Clean up data

Add information about fan size and fan type.

# change rt from s into ms

testing_all$rt <- testing_all$rt * 1000

#testing_all <- testing_all %>% filter(rt > 0)

testing_all$fan <- ifelse(testing_all$fan_loc == 4 | testing_all$fan_pers == 4, 1, -1)
testing_all$type_fan <- NA
testing_all$type_fan <- ifelse(testing_all$fan == 0, 0, ifelse(testing_all$fan_pers == 4, 1, -1))
testing_all <- testing_all %>% group_by(test_sentence) %>% mutate(pers_word=as.character(strsplit(test_sentence, " ")[[1]][2]), loc_word=as.character(strsplit(test_sentence, " ")[[1]][6]))
testing_all$pp_num <- as.numeric(testing_all$pp_num)
testing_all$manipulated_fan <- ifelse(testing_all$pp_num %% 2 == 1, "location", "person" )
testing_all$pp_num <- as.character(testing_all$pp_num)
testing_all$fan_word <- ifelse(testing_all$manipulated_fan == "person", testing_all$pers_word, testing_all$loc_word)
testing_all$fan_foil <- ifelse(testing_all$condition == "target", 0, ifelse(testing_all$fan == 1, 1, -1))
testing_all$fan_target <- ifelse(testing_all$condition == "foil", 0, ifelse(testing_all$fan == 1, 1, -1))
testing_all$type_condition <- ifelse(testing_all$condition == "target", 1, -1)

print(testing_all, width=Inf)
## # A tibble: 14,400 × 22
## # Groups:   test_sentence [2,608]
##     ...1 pp_num   yob education testing_rep
##    <dbl> <chr>  <dbl> <chr>           <dbl>
##  1     0 1       1995 HBO                 1
##  2     1 1       1995 HBO                 1
##  3     2 1       1995 HBO                 1
##  4     3 1       1995 HBO                 1
##  5     4 1       1995 HBO                 1
##  6     5 1       1995 HBO                 1
##  7     6 1       1995 HBO                 1
##  8     7 1       1995 HBO                 1
##  9     8 1       1995 HBO                 1
## 10     9 1       1995 HBO                 1
##    test_sentence                   response correct    rt fan_pers fan_loc
##    <chr>                           <chr>      <dbl> <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl>
##  1 De verkoper is in de plantenkas j              1 2842.        2       2
##  2 De goochelaar is in het terras  j              1 1908.        2       2
##  3 De onderzoeker is in de tram    j              1 2796.        2       4
##  4 De kruidenier is in de metro    j              1 1908.        2       4
##  5 De journalist is in de winkel   f              1 2012.        2       4
##  6 De verkoper is in de synagoge   f              1 3487.        2       2
##  7 De schilder is in de afgrond    j              1 2166.        2       2
##  8 De advocaat is in de bus        f              1 1453.        2       4
##  9 De kapitein is in de bus        j              1 1223.        2       4
## 10 De professor is in het filmhuis f              1 2057.        2       4
##    condition charcount   fan type_fan pers_word   loc_word  
##    <chr>         <int> <dbl>    <dbl> <chr>       <chr>     
##  1 target           31    -1       -1 verkoper    plantenkas
##  2 target           30    -1       -1 goochelaar  terras    
##  3 target           28     1       -1 onderzoeker tram      
##  4 target           28     1       -1 kruidenier  metro     
##  5 foil             29     1       -1 journalist  winkel    
##  6 foil             29    -1       -1 verkoper    synagoge  
##  7 target           28    -1       -1 schilder    afgrond   
##  8 foil             24     1       -1 advocaat    bus       
##  9 target           24     1       -1 kapitein    bus       
## 10 foil             31     1       -1 professor   filmhuis  
##    manipulated_fan fan_word   fan_foil fan_target type_condition
##    <chr>           <chr>         <dbl>      <dbl>          <dbl>
##  1 location        plantenkas        0         -1              1
##  2 location        terras            0         -1              1
##  3 location        tram              0          1              1
##  4 location        metro             0          1              1
##  5 location        winkel            1          0             -1
##  6 location        synagoge         -1          0             -1
##  7 location        afgrond           0         -1              1
##  8 location        bus               1          0             -1
##  9 location        bus               0          1              1
## 10 location        filmhuis          1          0             -1
## # ℹ 14,390 more rows

Exclude unreasonably long and short trials.

testing_all <- testing_all %>% filter(rt <= 90000 & rt > 200)

Exclude people with accuracy lower than 65 percent.

testing_all <- testing_all %>% filter(rt > 0) # exclude rts that are 0
testing_all %>% group_by(pp_num) %>% summarize(accuracy = mean(correct)) %>% filter(accuracy <= 0.65)
## # A tibble: 3 × 2
##   pp_num accuracy
##   <chr>     <dbl>
## 1 43        0.606
## 2 65        0.562
## 3 90        0.618
testing_correct <-testing_all %>% filter(!(pp_num %in% c("43","65", "90"))) # excluding 43 and 65 because of low accuracy
testing_correct %>% group_by(pp_num) %>% summarize(accuracy = mean(correct))
## # A tibble: 97 × 2
##    pp_num accuracy
##    <chr>     <dbl>
##  1 1         0.958
##  2 10        0.889
##  3 100       0.931
##  4 11        0.938
##  5 12        0.896
##  6 13        0.917
##  7 14        0.972
##  8 15        0.979
##  9 16        0.826
## 10 17        0.931
## # ℹ 87 more rows
testing_correct %>% group_by(fan_pers, fan_loc) %>% summarise(accuracy = mean(correct), se=sd(correct)/sqrt(length(correct)))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'fan_pers'. You can override using
## the `.groups` argument.
## # A tibble: 3 × 4
## # Groups:   fan_pers [2]
##   fan_pers fan_loc accuracy      se
##      <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl>
## 1        2       2    0.927 0.00382
## 2        2       4    0.923 0.00393
## 3        4       2    0.918 0.00400
hist(testing_correct$rt, xlim = c(0, 15000), breaks = 500)

Exclude trials slower than 8.3 seconds (3 sample sd away from the sample mean).

mean(testing_all$rt) + 3 * sd(testing_all$rt)
## [1] 8294.884
testing_faster <- testing_all %>% filter(rt <= mean(testing_all$rt) + 3 * sd(testing_all$rt)) # keeping only trials with a rt below 3 st.d. away from the mean
testing_faster_correct <- testing_correct %>% filter(rt <= mean(testing_all$rt) + 3 * sd(testing_all$rt)) # keeping only trials with a rt below 3 st.d. away from the mean

length(testing_faster_correct$rt)/length(testing_all$rt)
## [1] 0.9600307
length(testing_correct$rt)/length(testing_all$rt)
## [1] 0.9723075
length(testing_faster_correct$rt)/length(testing_correct$rt)
## [1] 0.9873736

Merge data

We include the dot product + log5 (i.e., pmi, which relates to spreading activation) to individual target-context words.

dotproduct$fan_word <- dotproduct$Word
dotproduct$pp_num <- dotproduct$List

dotproduct <- dotproduct %>% group_by(pp_num, fan_word) %>% summarise(pmi = mean(Dot_product) + log(5))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'pp_num'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
dotproduct$pp_num <- as.character(dotproduct$pp_num)

print(dotproduct)
## # A tibble: 2,400 × 3
## # Groups:   pp_num [100]
##    pp_num fan_word   pmi
##    <chr>  <chr>    <dbl>
##  1 1      afgrond   3.64
##  2 1      bioscoop  2.42
##  3 1      boetiek   2.95
##  4 1      bus       2.63
##  5 1      camper    3.29
##  6 1      caravan   2.84
##  7 1      cinema    3.05
##  8 1      dorp      2.31
##  9 1      filmhuis  3.23
## 10 1      gehucht   3.63
## # ℹ 2,390 more rows
merged_data <- inner_join(testing_faster_correct, dotproduct, by=c("fan_word", "pp_num"))

actr$fan_word <- actr$Word
actr$pp_num <- actr$List

actr <- actr %>% group_by(pp_num, fan_word) %>% summarise(actr = mean(ACT_R_activation), Location_fan=first(Location_fan))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'pp_num'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
# rescale activations, could also be for location and person separately
actr$actr <- scale(actr$actr)
#actr$actr[which(actr$Location_fan==TRUE)] <- scale(actr$actr[which(actr$Location_fan==TRUE)])
#actr$actr[which(actr$Location_fan==FALSE)] <- scale(actr$actr[which(actr$Location_fan==FALSE)])

actr$pp_num <- as.character(actr$pp_num)

print(actr)
## # A tibble: 2,400 × 4
## # Groups:   pp_num [100]
##    pp_num fan_word actr[,1] Location_fan
##    <chr>  <chr>       <dbl> <lgl>       
##  1 1      afgrond     0.868 TRUE        
##  2 1      bioscoop   -0.949 TRUE        
##  3 1      boetiek     0.201 TRUE        
##  4 1      bus        -0.700 TRUE        
##  5 1      camper      2.15  TRUE        
##  6 1      caravan     0.886 TRUE        
##  7 1      cinema      1.08  TRUE        
##  8 1      dorp       -1.88  TRUE        
##  9 1      filmhuis    1.58  TRUE        
## 10 1      gehucht    -0.392 TRUE        
## # ℹ 2,390 more rows
merged_data <- inner_join(merged_data, actr, by=c("fan_word", "pp_num"))

print(merged_data)
## # A tibble: 13,763 × 25
## # Groups:   test_sentence [2,571]
##     ...1 pp_num   yob education testing_rep test_sentence response correct
##    <dbl> <chr>  <dbl> <chr>           <dbl> <chr>         <chr>      <dbl>
##  1     0 1       1995 HBO                 1 De verkoper … j              1
##  2     1 1       1995 HBO                 1 De goochelaa… j              1
##  3     2 1       1995 HBO                 1 De onderzoek… j              1
##  4     3 1       1995 HBO                 1 De kruidenie… j              1
##  5     4 1       1995 HBO                 1 De journalis… f              1
##  6     5 1       1995 HBO                 1 De verkoper … f              1
##  7     6 1       1995 HBO                 1 De schilder … j              1
##  8     7 1       1995 HBO                 1 De advocaat … f              1
##  9     8 1       1995 HBO                 1 De kapitein … j              1
## 10     9 1       1995 HBO                 1 De professor… f              1
## # ℹ 13,753 more rows
## # ℹ 17 more variables: rt <dbl>, fan_pers <dbl>, fan_loc <dbl>,
## #   condition <chr>, charcount <int>, fan <dbl>, type_fan <dbl>,
## #   pers_word <chr>, loc_word <chr>, manipulated_fan <chr>,
## #   fan_word <chr>, fan_foil <dbl>, fan_target <dbl>,
## #   type_condition <dbl>, pmi <dbl>, actr <dbl[,1]>, Location_fan <lgl>

We subset by target (because these dot products were calculated on trained target-context pairs, and only targets were trained).

merged_data <- subset(merged_data, condition == "target")

# some quick checks using lme4
library(lme4)

m1 <- lmer(log(rt) ~ pmi + (1|pp_num) + (1|fan_word), data=merged_data)
print(summary(m1))
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: log(rt) ~ pmi + (1 | pp_num) + (1 | fan_word)
##    Data: merged_data
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 9418.7
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.3715 -0.6851 -0.2006  0.5389  3.9184 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  fan_word (Intercept) 0.005569 0.07463 
##  pp_num   (Intercept) 0.075684 0.27511 
##  Residual             0.216734 0.46555 
## Number of obs: 6873, groups:  fan_word, 123; pp_num, 97
## 
## Fixed effects:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)  7.54623    0.06160 122.506
## pmi         -0.05305    0.01895  -2.799
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##     (Intr)
## pmi -0.879
m2 <- lmer(log(rt) ~ pmi + fan + (1|pp_num) + (1|fan_word), data=merged_data)
print(summary(m2))
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: log(rt) ~ pmi + fan + (1 | pp_num) + (1 | fan_word)
##    Data: merged_data
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 9422.8
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.3944 -0.6864 -0.1986  0.5386  3.9051 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  fan_word (Intercept) 0.005387 0.0734  
##  pp_num   (Intercept) 0.075684 0.2751  
##  Residual             0.216701 0.4655  
## Number of obs: 6873, groups:  fan_word, 123; pp_num, 97
## 
## Fixed effects:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)  7.516317   0.063187 118.953
## pmi         -0.043680   0.019450  -2.246
## fan          0.013265   0.006512   2.037
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##     (Intr) pmi   
## pmi -0.885       
## fan -0.229  0.232
m2.1 <- lmer(log(rt) ~ actr + fan + (1|pp_num) + (1|fan_word), data=merged_data)
print(summary(m2.1))
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: log(rt) ~ actr + fan + (1 | pp_num) + (1 | fan_word)
##    Data: merged_data
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 9428.4
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.4025 -0.6825 -0.1967  0.5418  3.8949 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  fan_word (Intercept) 0.005027 0.0709  
##  pp_num   (Intercept) 0.075875 0.2755  
##  Residual             0.216964 0.4658  
## Number of obs: 6873, groups:  fan_word, 123; pp_num, 97
## 
## Fixed effects:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept) 7.390238   0.029411 251.274
## actr        0.008692   0.008840   0.983
## fan         0.019316   0.006845   2.822
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##      (Intr) actr  
## actr -0.024       
## fan  -0.059  0.383
m3 <- lmer(log(rt) ~ pmi + actr + fan + (1|pp_num) + (1|fan_word), data=merged_data)
print(summary(m3))
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: log(rt) ~ pmi + actr + fan + (1 | pp_num) + (1 | fan_word)
##    Data: merged_data
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 9428.2
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.3927 -0.6859 -0.1999  0.5301  3.8702 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  fan_word (Intercept) 0.005052 0.07108 
##  pp_num   (Intercept) 0.075936 0.27556 
##  Residual             0.216784 0.46560 
## Number of obs: 6873, groups:  fan_word, 123; pp_num, 97
## 
## Fixed effects:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)  7.531884   0.064033 117.625
## pmi         -0.049407   0.019835  -2.491
## actr         0.013389   0.009047   1.480
## fan          0.016856   0.006914   2.438
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##      (Intr) pmi    actr  
## pmi  -0.888              
## actr  0.175 -0.210       
## fan  -0.153  0.142  0.341
#resid(m1)

mbase <- lmer(log(rt) ~ fan + (1|pp_num) + (1|fan_word), data=merged_data)
print(summary(mbase))
## Linear mixed model fit by REML ['lmerMod']
## Formula: log(rt) ~ fan + (1 | pp_num) + (1 | fan_word)
##    Data: merged_data
## 
## REML criterion at convergence: 9421.8
## 
## Scaled residuals: 
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -3.4026 -0.6864 -0.1980  0.5414  3.9156 
## 
## Random effects:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  fan_word (Intercept) 0.005209 0.07217 
##  pp_num   (Intercept) 0.075703 0.27514 
##  Residual             0.216898 0.46572 
## Number of obs: 6873, groups:  fan_word, 123; pp_num, 97
## 
## Fixed effects:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value
## (Intercept)  7.39084    0.02940 251.393
## fan          0.01670    0.00633   2.638
## 
## Correlation of Fixed Effects:
##     (Intr)
## fan -0.053
m4 <- lm(resid(mbase) ~ pmi , data=merged_data)
print(summary(m4))
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = resid(mbase) ~ pmi, data = merged_data)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -1.58262 -0.32017 -0.09155  0.25030  1.81026 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)  
## (Intercept)  0.09199    0.04748   1.937   0.0527 .
## pmi         -0.03229    0.01655  -1.951   0.0511 .
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.4603 on 6871 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.0005536,  Adjusted R-squared:  0.0004081 
## F-statistic: 3.806 on 1 and 6871 DF,  p-value: 0.05112

Bayesian modeling

priors <- c(
  prior(normal(0, 15), class = Intercept),
  prior(normal(0, 2), class = b),
  prior(normal(0, 2), class = sigma),
  prior(normal(0, 2), class = sd),
  prior(lkj(2), class = cor)
)

priors_binom <- c(
  prior(normal(0, 2), class = Intercept),
  prior(normal(0, 1), class = b),
  prior(normal(0, 1), class = sd),
  prior(lkj(2), class = cor)
)

modeling <- function(useddata, measure, effect, adaptdelta=FALSE) {

  if (adaptdelta==FALSE) {
fit_model <- brm(
    paste0(measure, "~ 1 + ",effect," + fan + (1 + fan|pp_num) + (1 + fan|fan_word) "),# or (1|pers_word) + (1|loc_word)
  data = useddata,
  family = shifted_lognormal(),
  prior = priors,
  iter = 3000,
  cores = 4
)
  } else {
fit_model <- brm(
    paste0(measure, "~ 1 + ",effect," + fan + (1 + fan|pp_num) + (1 + fan|fan_word)"),# or (1|pers_word) + (1|loc_word)
  data = useddata,
  family = shifted_lognormal(),
  prior = priors,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.95),
  iter = 3000,
  cores = 4
)
  }

# output of the model

fit_model

}

# full random effects structure

modeling_full <- function(useddata, measure, effect, adaptdelta=FALSE) {

  if (adaptdelta==FALSE) {
fit_model <- brm(
    paste0(measure, "~ 1 + ",effect," + fan  + (1 + fan|pp_num) + (1 + ",effect," + fan|fan_word)"),# or (1|pers_word) + (1|loc_word)
  data = useddata,
  family = shifted_lognormal(),
  prior = priors,
  iter = 3000,
  cores = 4
)
  } else {
fit_model <- brm(
    paste0(measure, "~ 1 + ",effect," + fan  + (1 + fan|pp_num) + (1 + ",effect," + fan|fan_word)"),# or (1|pers_word) + (1|loc_word)
  data = useddata,
  family = shifted_lognormal(),
  prior = priors,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.999),
  iter = 3000,
  cores = 4
)
  }

# output of the model

fit_model

}

modeling_binom <- function(useddata, measure, effect, adaptdelta=FALSE) {

  if (adaptdelta==FALSE) {
    fit_model <- brm(paste0(measure, "~ 1 + ",effect," + fan  + (1 + ",effect," + fan|pp_num) + (1 + ",effect," + fan|fan_word)"),
  data = useddata,
  family = "bernoulli",
  prior = priors_binom,
  iter = 3000,
  cores = 4
)
  } else {
fit_model <- brm(paste0(measure, "~ 1 + ",effect," + fan  + (1 + ",effect," + fan|pp_num) + (1 + ",effect," + fan|fan_word)"),
  data = useddata,
  family = "bernoulli",
  prior = priors_binom,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.95),
  iter = 3000,
  cores = 4
)
  }

# output of the model

fit_model

}

Modeling

Accuracy models

Accuracy model 1

#fan_accuracy <- modeling_binom(merged_data, "correct", adaptdelta = TRUE)
#save(fan_accuracy, file="../stan_models/fan_accuracy.Rdata")
load("../stan_models/fan_accuracy.Rdata")

print(fan_accuracy, digits=3)
## Warning: Parts of the model have not converged (some Rhats are > 1.05).
## Be careful when analysing the results! We recommend running more
## iterations and/or setting stronger priors.
## Warning: There were 619 divergent transitions after warmup. Increasing
## adapt_delta above 0.95 may help. See
## http://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings.html#divergent-transitions-after-warmup
##  Family: bernoulli 
##   Links: mu = logit 
## Formula: correct ~ 1 + pmi + fan + (1 + pmi + fan | pp_num) + (1 + pmi + fan | fan_word) 
##    Data: useddata (Number of observations: 6873) 
##   Draws: 4 chains, each with iter = 3000; warmup = 1500; thin = 1;
##          total post-warmup draws = 6000
## 
## Multilevel Hyperparameters:
## ~fan_word (Number of levels: 123) 
##                    Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI  Rhat Bulk_ESS
## sd(Intercept)         0.201     0.161    0.011    0.512 1.007      573
## sd(pmi)               0.072     0.059    0.005    0.186 1.034      117
## sd(fan)               0.223     0.097    0.030    0.409 1.011      746
## cor(Intercept,pmi)   -0.128     0.418   -0.875    0.691 1.006      713
## cor(Intercept,fan)   -0.078     0.398   -0.750    0.681 1.022      148
## cor(pmi,fan)         -0.042     0.367   -0.727    0.683 1.019     1130
##                    Tail_ESS
## sd(Intercept)          1714
## sd(pmi)                 700
## sd(fan)                1115
## cor(Intercept,pmi)     1709
## cor(Intercept,fan)     1512
## cor(pmi,fan)           2252
## 
## ~pp_num (Number of levels: 97) 
##                    Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI  Rhat Bulk_ESS
## sd(Intercept)         1.761     0.711    0.502    3.073 1.059       67
## sd(pmi)               0.561     0.270    0.039    1.031 1.107       36
## sd(fan)               0.441     0.076    0.297    0.597 1.003     1488
## cor(Intercept,pmi)   -0.674     0.380   -0.952    0.465 1.085       45
## cor(Intercept,fan)   -0.251     0.227   -0.653    0.244 1.007      490
## cor(pmi,fan)          0.017     0.280   -0.606    0.520 1.043      280
##                    Tail_ESS
## sd(Intercept)          1197
## sd(pmi)                  86
## sd(fan)                2510
## cor(Intercept,pmi)      226
## cor(Intercept,fan)     1167
## cor(pmi,fan)            182
## 
## Regression Coefficients:
##           Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI  Rhat Bulk_ESS Tail_ESS
## Intercept    2.066     0.448    1.204    2.967 1.022      971     2585
## pmi          0.162     0.154   -0.138    0.459 1.028     1090     2870
## fan         -0.150     0.081   -0.317    0.003 1.008      863     3157
## 
## Draws were sampled using sampling(NUTS). For each parameter, Bulk_ESS
## and Tail_ESS are effective sample size measures, and Rhat is the potential
## scale reduction factor on split chains (at convergence, Rhat = 1).
pp_check(fan_accuracy, ndraws=1000)

pp_check(fan_accuracy, ndraws=1000, type = "stat", stat = "mean")
## `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`.

RT models

  • using faster+correct subset
  • only looking at dataset with RTs smaller than 10.8 s and accuracy higher than 60 per cent.

RT model 1 - maximal working random effects

I removed pmi for ppnum because that model had problems to converge even after increased adapt delta and maxtreedepth.

fan_together_full <- modeling_full(merged_data, "rt", "pmi", adaptdelta=TRUE)
## Compiling Stan program...
## Start sampling
## Warning: Bulk Effective Samples Size (ESS) is too low, indicating posterior means and medians may be unreliable.
## Running the chains for more iterations may help. See
## https://mc-stan.org/misc/warnings.html#bulk-ess
save(fan_together_full, file="../stan_models/fan_together_full.Rdata")
load("../stan_models/fan_together_full.Rdata")

print(fan_together_full, digits=3)
##  Family: shifted_lognormal 
##   Links: mu = identity; sigma = identity; ndt = identity 
## Formula: rt ~ 1 + pmi + fan + (1 + fan | pp_num) + (1 + pmi + fan | fan_word) 
##    Data: useddata (Number of observations: 6873) 
##   Draws: 4 chains, each with iter = 3000; warmup = 1500; thin = 1;
##          total post-warmup draws = 6000
## 
## Multilevel Hyperparameters:
## ~fan_word (Number of levels: 123) 
##                    Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI  Rhat Bulk_ESS
## sd(Intercept)         0.074     0.038    0.008    0.161 1.004     1040
## sd(pmi)               0.020     0.013    0.001    0.052 1.012      275
## sd(fan)               0.040     0.015    0.007    0.067 1.002     1056
## cor(Intercept,pmi)   -0.151     0.431   -0.883    0.698 1.003     1302
## cor(Intercept,fan)   -0.004     0.350   -0.666    0.701 1.005     1280
## cor(pmi,fan)         -0.042     0.378   -0.750    0.701 1.008     1216
##                    Tail_ESS
## sd(Intercept)          1111
## sd(pmi)                1221
## sd(fan)                1236
## cor(Intercept,pmi)     2733
## cor(Intercept,fan)     1549
## cor(pmi,fan)           2154
## 
## ~pp_num (Number of levels: 97) 
##                    Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI  Rhat Bulk_ESS
## sd(Intercept)         0.326     0.025    0.282    0.383 1.002     1163
## sd(fan)               0.055     0.011    0.033    0.075 1.001     2013
## cor(Intercept,fan)   -0.135     0.162   -0.446    0.192 1.001     6419
##                    Tail_ESS
## sd(Intercept)          2179
## sd(fan)                2910
## cor(Intercept,fan)     4994
## 
## Regression Coefficients:
##           Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI  Rhat Bulk_ESS Tail_ESS
## Intercept    7.364     0.074    7.220    7.511 1.001     2022     3591
## pmi         -0.047     0.023   -0.092   -0.003 1.000    11811     4723
## fan          0.016     0.010   -0.004    0.036 1.000     6567     5148
## 
## Further Distributional Parameters:
##       Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI  Rhat Bulk_ESS Tail_ESS
## sigma    0.530     0.005    0.520    0.540 1.000     8267     4833
## ndt    210.816     4.794  200.950  219.585 1.000    10794     4658
## 
## Draws were sampled using sampling(NUTS). For each parameter, Bulk_ESS
## and Tail_ESS are effective sample size measures, and Rhat is the potential
## scale reduction factor on split chains (at convergence, Rhat = 1).
pp_check(fan_together_full, ndraws=1000)

pp_check(fan_together_full, ndraws=1000, type = "stat", stat = "mean")
## `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`.

pp_check(fan_together_full, ndraws=1000, type = "stat", stat = "median")
## `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`.

RT model 2 - maximal working random effects

fan_together_full_actr <- modeling_full(merged_data, "rt", "actr", adaptdelta=TRUE)
## Compiling Stan program...
## Start sampling
save(fan_together_full_actr, file="../stan_models/fan_together_full_actr.Rdata")
load("../stan_models/fan_together_full_actr.Rdata")

print(fan_together_full_actr, digits=3)
##  Family: shifted_lognormal 
##   Links: mu = identity; sigma = identity; ndt = identity 
## Formula: rt ~ 1 + actr + fan + (1 + fan | pp_num) + (1 + actr + fan | fan_word) 
##    Data: useddata (Number of observations: 6873) 
##   Draws: 4 chains, each with iter = 3000; warmup = 1500; thin = 1;
##          total post-warmup draws = 6000
## 
## Multilevel Hyperparameters:
## ~fan_word (Number of levels: 123) 
##                     Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI  Rhat Bulk_ESS
## sd(Intercept)          0.069     0.014    0.039    0.096 1.004     1120
## sd(actr)               0.052     0.020    0.011    0.089 1.006      765
## sd(fan)                0.038     0.016    0.004    0.068 1.017      567
## cor(Intercept,actr)    0.105     0.281   -0.424    0.666 1.001     1467
## cor(Intercept,fan)     0.007     0.295   -0.560    0.608 1.002     2246
## cor(actr,fan)         -0.002     0.360   -0.703    0.671 1.002     1371
##                     Tail_ESS
## sd(Intercept)           1390
## sd(actr)                1122
## sd(fan)                  687
## cor(Intercept,actr)     2826
## cor(Intercept,fan)      3047
## cor(actr,fan)           2342
## 
## ~pp_num (Number of levels: 97) 
##                    Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI  Rhat Bulk_ESS
## sd(Intercept)         0.327     0.025    0.282    0.379 1.002      816
## sd(fan)               0.054     0.011    0.033    0.076 1.002     1672
## cor(Intercept,fan)   -0.162     0.163   -0.474    0.167 1.001     5374
##                    Tail_ESS
## sd(Intercept)          1761
## sd(fan)                1864
## cor(Intercept,fan)     4490
## 
## Regression Coefficients:
##           Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI  Rhat Bulk_ESS Tail_ESS
## Intercept    7.226     0.035    7.156    7.294 1.002      460      595
## actr         0.011     0.012   -0.013    0.035 1.000     4079     4381
## fan          0.023     0.010    0.003    0.044 1.000     4519     4427
## 
## Further Distributional Parameters:
##       Estimate Est.Error l-95% CI u-95% CI  Rhat Bulk_ESS Tail_ESS
## sigma    0.530     0.005    0.520    0.541 1.000     4751     4646
## ndt    210.907     4.863  200.482  219.670 1.000     7216     4318
## 
## Draws were sampled using sampling(NUTS). For each parameter, Bulk_ESS
## and Tail_ESS are effective sample size measures, and Rhat is the potential
## scale reduction factor on split chains (at convergence, Rhat = 1).
pp_check(fan_together_full_actr, ndraws=1000)

pp_check(fan_together_full_actr, ndraws=1000, type = "stat", stat = "mean")
## `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`.

pp_check(fan_together_full_actr, ndraws=1000, type = "stat", stat = "median")
## `stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`.
